english: Lex., bra-ril (bra ril) Cs., dung of it; bra-lpágs (bra lpags) skin of it. - 2. vb. to have or to be in great plenty, to abound (?), ráṅ-gis za ma bra (rang gis za ma bra), btuṅ ma bra (btung ma bra), gon ma bra (gon ma bra) she allowed herself no abundance of food, drink, or clothing; *za-, tuṅ-, čín-, lab-, zér-dha-te* (*za-, tung-, cin-, lab-, zer-dha-te*) eating, drinking plentifully, walking, speaking, talking a great deal C.; *tsa-, ḍho-, ḍháṅ-dha-te* (*tsa-, dho-, dhang-dha-te*) being very hot, warm, cold C.
(prov. *brau*, Pur. *bro*) buckwheat; bra-pyé (bra phye) Lex., rjen (rjen) Sch. buckwheat flour; bra-sóg (bra sog) buck-wheat straw, serving as a poor sort of fodder during winter.

rock, brag rtse-gsum-pa (brag rtse gsum pa) a three-pointed rock; brag-skéd (brag sked) the middle height of a rock, opp. to brag-mjug (brag mjug) and rtse (rtse) its foot and top Cs. - brag-spós (brag spos) prob. an aromatic herb, used for incense Lt. - brag-skíbs (brag skibs) beetling rock. - brag-rgyál (brag rgyal) a prominent, high and precipitous rock, towering rock. - brág-ca, -ča (brag ca, brag cha) echo; also fig. for something unsubstantial, shadowy, not existing Mil. - brag-mjúg (brag mjug) foot of a rock Cs. - brag-púg (brag phug) rock-cavern. - brag-pyé (brag phye) dust produced by hewing stones Glr. - *ḍhag-bóṅ* (*dhag-bong*) = ṕa-bóṅ (pha bong) C. - brag-dmár (brag dmar) name of a rock in or near Lhasa, alledged not to be identic with dmar-po-ri (dmar po ri)(Sch.). - *ḍag-tsél-wa, ḍag-šig-pa* (*dag-tsel-wa, dag-shig-pa*) mite, tick W. - brag-rtsáṅ (brag rtsang) rock-lizard. - brag-rtsé (brag rtse) top of a rock. - brag-žún (brag zhun) mineral pitch, bitumen, is said to cure fevers and even fractures. - brag-rí (brag ri) rocky hill. - brag-rúd (brag rud) fall of a rock. - brag-róṅ (brag rong) chasm in a rock, ravine. - brag-šig (brag shig) v. brag-rtsél-ba (brag rtsel ba).

1. resp. sku-bráṅ (sku brang) chest, breast, (cf. nú-ma (nu ma)); braṅ rdúṅ-ba (brang rdung ba) to beat one's breast Glr.; *t'ú-gu ḍáṅ-la čir-te kyér-če* (*thu-gu dang-la cir-te kyer-che*) W. to carry a child pressed against one's breast. - braṅ-kyéd (brang kyed) (?) Cs. a high, prominent chest. - braṅ-dkýil (brang dkyil) middle of the breast, cardiac region. - braṅ-lkóg (brang lkog) Mil. prob. = lkóg-ma (lkog ma). - braṅ-skás (brang skas) Sch. the dorsal vertebrae opposite to the chest. - *ḍaṅ-kúd* (*dang-kud*) string of the braṅ-kúṅ (-guṅ, -koṅ, -goṅ) (brang kung (-gung, -kong, -gong)), pellet-bow, a bow furnished with two strings, to shoot pellets or small stones, braṅ-rdi (brang rdi) or -rdeu (-rdeu), with it W. - *ḍhaṅ-kóg* (*dhang-kog*) C. cardiac-region, pit of the stomach. - braṅ-sgró (brang sgro) snake, serpent (like lto-ºgró (lto hgro)). - braṅ-búr (brang bur) the middle convex part of the rdó-rje (rdo rje) Ma. - braṅ-tsig (brang tsig) Lh., prob. heart-burning. - braṅ-(y)žól (brang (g)zhol) Cs. dew-lap. - braṅ-ze (brang ze) Mil. prob. breast-bone, sternum. - braṅ-yyúṅ (brang g.yung) Sch. tame, gentle. - braṅ-rus (brang rus) Med. breast-bone. - *ḍaṅ-lág* (*dang-lag*) W. the hands crossed on the breast. - braṅ-so (brang so) Glr. breast, brisket of a butchered animal. - 2. also ºbraṅ (hbrang), gen. ºbráṅ-sa (hbrang sa), eleg. mčis-bráṅ (mchis brang) (q.v.), resp. yzim-bráṅ (gzims brang), bžugs-bráṅ (bzhugs brang) night-quarters, halting-place, whether under a roof or in the open air; also as much as stage (of a journey); bráṅ-sa ºdébs-pa (brang sa hdebs pa) Tar., prob. also *bór-če* (*bor-che*) W., to take up night-quarters; dwelling, particularly a temporary one, lodgings; but also a permanent abode, esp. in W.; *ḍaṅ-sa táṅ-če, yár-ce* (*dang-sa tang-che, yar-ce*) to take in, to lodge a person over night W. (cf. gnas 2 (gnas 2)). - bráṅ-k'aṅ (brang khang), dwelling-house, dwelling-room Pth. - braṅ-grógs (brang grogs) house-mate, bed-fellow. - braṅ-dpón (brang dpon) master of the house, landlord. - p'o-bráṅ (pho brang) v. p'o (pho); bla-bráṅ (bla brang) v. bla (bla).

1. slave, servant, mi-brán (mi bran) 'vir servus' S.g.; bran byéd-pa (bran byed pa) to be a servant, to serve Cs.; brán-du gyúr-ba (bran du gyur ba) to become a servant Cs.; brán-du ºkól-ba (bran du hkol ba) to make another be a servant, to use him as a servant B.; brán-du skúl-ba (bran du skul ba) to engage a person as a servant, to get him to work for one's self Glr.; brán-du kas-bláṅs-so (bran du khas blangs so) Pth. they promised to serve him; lus ṅag yid gsum brán-du púl-te (lus ngag yid gsum bran du phul te) devoting heart, mouth, and body to his service Pth.; naṅ nub lto-gós-kyis brán-du kól (nang nub lto gos kyis bran du khol) morning and night I am a slave to food and clothing Mil.; subject, one owing allegiance, *la-dágs-si gyál-po-la ḍán-yul-tso* (*la-dags-si gyal-po-la dan-yul-tso*) a village subject to, belonging to, the king of Ladak W.; brán-pó (bran po) servant, slave Tar.; brán-mo (bran mo) maid-servant, female slave; bran-kól (bran khol), bran-yyóg (bran g.yog) = bran (bran); also collectively, servants, domestics, household. - 2. texture, in the
